Economy

131 solved questions in this topic · SSC GD General Knowledge & General Awareness

Home
Q.No 27 EconomyEnglish
The Ease of Doing Business (EODB) indicator “Enforcing Contracts” measures:
A
implementation of labor regulations
B
Ease of paying and managing taxes
C
Efficiency of the judicial system in resolving commercial disputes
✓ Correct
D
Effectiveness of law enforcement agencies

Solution

Correct answer: C. In the World Bank's Ease of Doing Business framework, the 'Enforcing Contracts' indicator measures the time, cost and procedural quality of the judicial system in resolving a standardised commercial dispute.

A is incorrect: Labour-regulation implementation is captured by separate labour-market indicators, not Enforcing Contracts.

B is incorrect: Ease of paying and managing taxes is measured by the 'Paying Taxes' indicator.

D is incorrect: Effectiveness of law-enforcement (police) agencies is a governance/security measure, not part of this EODB indicator.

💡 The World Bank discontinued the Doing Business report in 2021 and is replacing it with the Business Ready (B-READY) project.
Q.No 31 EconomyEnglish
What was the main drawback of small-scale industry reservation policies?
A
They encouraged monopoly power
B
They eliminated export opportunities
C
They excluded rural entrepreneurs
D
They restricted economies of scale and technological upgrading
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Reserving products exclusively for small-scale industries kept firms artificially small, which restricted economies of scale and discouraged investment in modern technology, hurting productivity and competitiveness.

A is incorrect: The policy was meant to prevent monopoly by protecting small firms, not to encourage monopoly power.

B is incorrect: It did not specifically eliminate export opportunities; the core problem was scale and technology.

C is incorrect: Reservation did not exclude rural entrepreneurs; in fact small units were widely spread.

💡 India progressively 'de-reserved' SSI products after 1997, and the reservation list was abolished entirely by 2015.
Q.No 32 EconomyEnglish
Which was the model for the First Five-Year Plan (1951-1956)?
A
Solow model
B
Harrod-Domar model
✓ Correct
C
Keynesian model
D
Mahalanobis model

Solution

Correct answer: B. India's First Five-Year Plan (1951-56) was based on the Harrod-Domar model, which links growth to the rate of saving and investment and the capital-output ratio.

A is incorrect: The Solow growth model came later and was not the basis of the First Plan.

C is incorrect: The Keynesian model focuses on demand management, not the planning framework used here.

D is incorrect: The Mahalanobis model was the basis of the Second Five-Year Plan (1956-61), not the First.

💡 The First Plan prioritised agriculture and irrigation following the disruptions of Partition.
Q.No 35 EconomyEnglish
Which financial institution plays the lead role in supporting India’s export and import activities?
A
NABARD
B
EXIM Bank
✓ Correct
C
World Bank
D
SIDBI

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Export-Import (EXIM) Bank of India is the apex financial institution set up in 1982 specifically to finance and promote India's foreign trade.

A is incorrect: NABARD is the apex bank for agriculture and rural development.

C is incorrect: The World Bank is an international multilateral lender, not India's export-import bank.

D is incorrect: SIDBI is the principal institution for financing small and medium enterprises.

💡 EXIM Bank also extends Lines of Credit on behalf of the Government of India to other developing countries.
Q.No 38 EconomyEnglish
As per a report by Prime Minister’s Economic Advisory Panel EAC-PM, the number of domestic migrants have lowered by nearly between 2011 and 2023.
A
12%
✓ Correct
B
9%
C
15%
D
21%

Solution

Correct answer: A. According to the EAC-PM (Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister) analysis, domestic migration fell by nearly 12% between 2011 and 2023, indicating greater local economic opportunity.

B is incorrect: 9% understates the decline reported.

C is incorrect: 15% is higher than the figure cited in the report.

D is incorrect: 21% overstates the reported fall in migration.

💡 The EAC-PM is a non-constitutional advisory body that gives the Prime Minister independent economic advice.
Q.No 26 EconomyHindi
निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा खुशी सूचकांक में उदारता कारक का उदाहरण है?
A
राष्ट्रीय कर दरें
B
विदेशी सहायता
C
सरकारी सब्सिडी
D
दान देने या दूसरों की मदद करने की इच्छा
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. In the World Happiness Report, 'Generosity' is measured by people's willingness to donate money or help others, reflecting prosocial behaviour in a society.

A is incorrect: National tax rates are a fiscal-policy variable, not a measure of generosity.

B is incorrect: Foreign aid is given between governments and is not the generosity factor of the index.

C is incorrect: Government subsidies are state transfers, not the voluntary giving that generosity captures.

💡 The World Happiness Report ranks countries using six key factors, including GDP per capita, social support and generosity.
Q.No 29 EconomyHindi
99 से पहले किस नीति ने कुछ उद्योगों में निजी क्षेत्र की भागीदारी को सीमित कर दिया था?
A
Industrial Licensing Policy
✓ Correct
B
Agricultural Subsidy Policy
C
Foreign Trade Policy
D
Public Distribution System

Solution

Correct answer: A. Before the 1991 reforms, the Industrial Licensing Policy (the 'Licence Raj', based on the Industries (Development and Regulation) Act, 1951) restricted private-sector entry by requiring government licences and reserving many industries for the public sector.

B is incorrect: Agricultural subsidy policy supports farmers and does not restrict private industry.

C is incorrect: Foreign trade policy governs exports and imports, not domestic industrial entry.

D is incorrect: The Public Distribution System distributes subsidised food grains, unrelated to industrial licensing.

💡 The 1991 New Industrial Policy abolished licensing for most industries, opening the economy to private and foreign investment.
Q.No 33 EconomyHindi
राष्ट्रीय नमूना सर्वेक्षण संगठन (/550) की स्थापना किस उद्देश्य के लिए की गई थी?
A
कृषि को बढ़ावा देना
B
उद्योग पर नज़र रखना
C
निर्यात को बढ़ावा देना
D
सामाजिक-ओआर्थिक स्थितियों पर सांख्यिकीय ऑआँकड़े एकत्र करना
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The National Sample Survey Organisation (NSSO) was set up to collect statistical data on socio-economic conditions through large-scale sample surveys, providing inputs for policy and the national accounts.

A is incorrect: Promoting agriculture is the work of bodies like ICAR/NABARD, not the NSSO.

B is incorrect: Monitoring industry through production indices is the Annual Survey of Industries / industrial statistics work, distinct from NSSO's socio-economic surveys.

C is incorrect: Promoting exports is the role of bodies like the DGFT and EXIM Bank.

💡 The NSSO is now part of the National Statistical Office (NSO) under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation.
Q.No 35 EconomyHindi
निम्नलिखित में से कौन सी चुनौती आज भारतीय कृषि मे प्रमुख है?
A
ग्रामीण क्षेत्रों में कम जनसंख्या घनत्व के कारण कृषि श्रम की कमी होना
B
कृषि मशीनीकरण का बढ़ता स्तर
C
कृषि जोतों का लगातार खंडित होना और परिचालन होल्डिंग्स के आकार में गिरावट
✓ Correct
D
मुख्य खाद्यान्नों की घटती घरेलू मांग

Solution

Correct answer: C. A major structural problem of Indian agriculture is the continuous fragmentation of landholdings due to inheritance, which steadily reduces the size of operational holdings and lowers efficiency.

A is incorrect: Indian agriculture suffers from surplus, under-employed labour, not a shortage caused by low rural population density.

B is incorrect: Rising mechanisation is a positive trend, not a core challenge.

D is incorrect: Domestic demand for staple food grains is not falling; the problem is on the supply/structure side.

💡 Average operational holding size in India has fallen to roughly 1.08 hectares, making most farmers small or marginal.
Q.No 36 EconomyHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
भारत के पहले निर्यात प्रसंस्करण क्षेत्र का स्थान किस भारतीय बंदरगाह को बनाया गया था?
A
सूरत
B
कांडला
✓ Correct
C
मुंबई
D
कोचीन

Solution

Correct answer: B. India's first Export Processing Zone (EPZ) was set up at Kandla, Gujarat, in 1965 - it was also Asia's first EPZ.

A is incorrect: Surat is a major textile/diamond hub but was not the site of the first EPZ.

C is incorrect: Mumbai's SEEPZ came later (1973), after Kandla.

D is incorrect: Cochin had an EPZ established later, not the first.

💡 Export Processing Zones were the forerunners of today's Special Economic Zones (SEZs).
Q.No 33 EconomyHindi
किस नीति ने लघु उद्योगों को प्रमुखता से बढ़ावा दिया?
A
Industrial Policy of 1948
B
Industrial Policy of 1956
C
Industrial Policy of 1977
✓ Correct
D
Industrial Policy of 1980

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Industrial Policy of 1977 (under the Janata government) gave special prominence to small-scale and cottage industries, expanding the list of items reserved for them and promoting decentralised development.

A is incorrect: The 1948 policy laid the first framework for a mixed economy but did not specially emphasise small industries.

B is incorrect: The 1956 policy emphasised heavy industry and the public sector ('commanding heights').

D is incorrect: The 1980 policy focused on raising productivity and reviving the public sector, not chiefly on small industry.

💡 The 1977 policy introduced the concept of 'District Industries Centres' to promote small enterprises.
Q.No 35 EconomyHindi
जब नाममात्र सकल घरेलू उत्पाद (८07?) वास्तविक जीडीपी (८0?) की तुलना में तेज़ी से बढ़ता है, तो यह __ दर्शाता है
A
शून्य वृद्धि
B
गिरती मुद्रास्फीति
C
उच्च मुद्रास्फीति
✓ Correct
D
अपस्फीति

Solution

Correct answer: C. If nominal GDP rises faster than real GDP, the gap is due to rising prices - this indicates high inflation, because nominal GDP is measured at current prices while real GDP is adjusted for price changes.

A is incorrect: Zero growth would mean GDP is not changing, which is not the case here.

B is incorrect: Falling inflation would narrow, not widen, the gap between nominal and real GDP.

D is incorrect: Deflation means falling prices, which would make nominal GDP grow slower than real GDP.

💡 The ratio of nominal to real GDP is the GDP deflator, a broad measure of economy-wide inflation.
Q.No 36 EconomyHindi
भारत के व्यापार संतुलन की मुख्य कमी क्या है?
A
सेवाओं का अधिशेष निर्यात
B
कुछ व्यापारिक साझेदारों पर अत्यधिक निर्भरता
C
आयात के कारण लगातार व्यापार घाटा
✓ Correct
D
उच्च शुल्क संरचना

Solution

Correct answer: C. The main weakness of India's balance of trade is a persistent trade deficit - the value of imports (especially crude oil, gold and electronics) consistently exceeds the value of exports.

A is incorrect: A surplus in services exports is actually a strength that partly offsets the goods deficit, not a weakness.

B is incorrect: Over-dependence on a few partners is a concern but not the defining weakness of the trade balance.

D is incorrect: A high tariff structure is a policy feature, not the core trade-balance problem.

💡 India's large software and services exports help finance its merchandise (goods) trade deficit in the current account.
Q.No 28 EconomyHindi
__मानव विकास सूचकांक (५०) एक समग्र सूचकांक है।
A
शिक्षा, आय, जीवन प्रत्याशा
✓ Correct
B
सकल घरेलू उत्पाद, सकल राष्ट्रीय आय, साक्षरता
C
रोजगार, सामाजिक सुरक्षा, लैंगिक समानता
D
आय, स्वास्थ्य, पर्यावरण

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Human Development Index (HDI) is a composite index based on three dimensions: education (knowledge), income (standard of living) and life expectancy (a long, healthy life).

B is incorrect: HDI does not combine GDP, GNI and literacy in that specific set; income is captured by GNI per capita while knowledge uses schooling, not just literacy.

C is incorrect: Employment, social security and gender equality are not the three HDI components.

D is incorrect: Environment is not a component of the standard HDI.

💡 The HDI was introduced by the UNDP in 1990, conceptualised by economists Mahbub ul Haq and Amartya Sen.
Q.No 29 EconomyEnglish
How many key variables are used to rank countries in the Happiness Index?
A
4
B
5
C
6
✓ Correct
D
7

Solution

Correct answer: C. The World Happiness Report uses six key variables to rank countries: GDP per capita, social support, healthy life expectancy, freedom to make life choices, generosity and perceptions of corruption.

A is incorrect: Four is fewer than the six factors used.

B is incorrect: Five omits one of the standard factors.

D is incorrect: Seven is one more than the six key variables.

💡 Finland has topped the World Happiness Report for several consecutive years.
Q.No 33 EconomyEnglish
Minimum Support Price (MSP) was introduced in:
A
1950s
B
1960s
✓ Correct
C
1970s
D
1980s

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Minimum Support Price (MSP) system was introduced in the 1960s (around 1966-67) during the Green Revolution to assure farmers a guaranteed price and encourage food-grain production.

A is incorrect: The 1950s pre-dates the MSP mechanism.

C is incorrect: The 1970s is after MSP had already been introduced.

D is incorrect: The 1980s is much later than the actual introduction.

💡 MSP is announced by the government on the recommendation of the Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP).
Q.No 36 EconomyEnglish
Which index measures wholesale prices?
A
Gross Domestic Product Deflator
B
Consumer Price Index
C
Index of Industrial Production
D
Wholesale Price Index
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The Wholesale Price Index (WPI) measures the average change in prices of goods at the wholesale level (bulk transactions between businesses).

A is incorrect: The GDP deflator measures economy-wide price change, not specifically wholesale prices.

B is incorrect: The Consumer Price Index (CPI) measures prices at the retail (consumer) level.

C is incorrect: The Index of Industrial Production (IIP) measures output/production volume, not prices.

💡 In India, WPI is released by the Office of the Economic Adviser, while CPI is released by the National Statistical Office.
Q.No 37 EconomyEnglish
Which structural constraint continues to be the most critical factor limiting agricultural productivity in India?
A
inadequacy of fertile soils across major regions
B
High dependence on monsoonal variability
✓ Correct
C
Chronic shortage of agricultural labor
D
Weakness of domestic market linkages

Solution

Correct answer: B. High dependence on the monsoon (rainfall variability) is the most critical structural constraint on Indian agricultural productivity, since a large share of farmland is rain-fed and vulnerable to erratic rains.

A is incorrect: India has substantial fertile soil; inadequacy of fertile soil is not the principal constraint.

C is incorrect: India generally has surplus agricultural labour, not a chronic shortage.

D is incorrect: Weak market linkages are a problem but not the most critical productivity constraint.

💡 About half of India's net sown area is still rain-fed, which is why irrigation expansion is a policy priority.
Q.No 29 EconomyHindi
दिवालियापन समाधान सूचक __मूल्यांकन करता है।
A
सरकारी ऋण स्तर
B
व्यवसायों के लिए बीमा कवर
C
दिवालियापन के मामलों में समय, लागत और वसूली दर
✓ Correct
D
ऋण ब्याज दरें

Solution

Correct answer: C. The 'Resolving Insolvency' indicator assesses the time taken, the cost involved, and the recovery rate achieved in resolving insolvency (bankruptcy) cases.

A is incorrect: Government debt levels are a fiscal measure, not part of this indicator.

B is incorrect: Insurance cover for businesses is unrelated to insolvency resolution.

D is incorrect: Loan interest rates are a monetary variable, not what this indicator measures.

💡 In India, the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC), 2016 sped up and streamlined insolvency resolution.
Q.No 33 EconomyHindi
भारत में “श्वेत क्रांति के जनक” के रूप में किसे जाना जाता है?
A
वर्गीस कुरियन
✓ Correct
B
विक्रम साराभाई
C
नॉर्मन बोरलॉग
D
एम.एस. स्वामीनाथन

Solution

Correct answer: A. Dr Verghese Kurien is known as the 'Father of the White Revolution' in India for leading Operation Flood, which made India the world's largest milk producer.

B is incorrect: Vikram Sarabhai is the father of the Indian space programme.

C is incorrect: Norman Borlaug is associated with the global Green Revolution in wheat.

D is incorrect: M.S. Swaminathan is called the father of India's Green Revolution.

💡 Kurien founded Amul and the cooperative dairy model at Anand, Gujarat.
Q.No 34 EconomyHindi
956 के औद्योगिक नीति प्रस्ताव ने उद्योगों को कितनी श्रेणियों में वर्गीकृत किया?
A
दो
B
तीन
✓ Correct
C
चार
D
पाँच

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Industrial Policy Resolution of 1956 classified industries into three categories (Schedules A, B and C), defining the roles of the public and private sectors.

A is incorrect: It used three categories, not two.

C is incorrect: There were three schedules, not four.

D is incorrect: Five is not the number of categories under the 1956 resolution.

💡 Schedule A reserved key industries for the State; the 1956 resolution gave the public sector the 'commanding heights' of the economy.
Q.No 36 EconomyHindi
यदि सरकार संकुचनकारी राजकोषीय नीति अपनाती है, तो____
A
सार्वजनिक व्यय को कम करता है या करों को बढ़ाता है।
✓ Correct
B
अधिक पैसा छापता है।
C
करों को कम करता है।
D
सार्वजनिक व्यय बढ़ाता है।

Solution

Correct answer: A. Under a contractionary fiscal policy the government reduces public expenditure and/or raises taxes to cut aggregate demand, typically to control inflation.

B is incorrect: Printing more money is monetary expansion by the central bank, not fiscal policy.

C is incorrect: Cutting taxes is an expansionary fiscal measure.

D is incorrect: Increasing public expenditure is also expansionary, the opposite of contractionary.

💡 Fiscal policy concerns government spending and taxation, while monetary policy concerns money supply and interest rates.
Q.No 37 EconomyHindi
भारतीय अर्थव्यवस्था का कौन सा क्षेत्र कृषि के बाद रोजगार का दूसरा सबसे बड़ा स्रोत है?
A
बड़े पैमाने पर पूंजी-गहन औद्योगिक उद्यम
B
सूक्ष्म, लघु और मध्यम उद्यम (एमएसएमई)
✓ Correct
C
सूचना प्रौद्योगिकी और सॉफ्टवेयर सेवाएँ
D
परिवहन और रसद बुनियादी ढांचे

Solution

Correct answer: B. After agriculture, the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME) sector is the second-largest source of employment in the Indian economy.

A is incorrect: Large capital-intensive industries employ relatively few people.

C is incorrect: IT and software services are high-value but employ far fewer than MSMEs.

D is incorrect: Transport and logistics infrastructure is not the second-largest employer.

💡 MSMEs contribute roughly 30% of India's GDP and a large share of its exports.
Q.No 38 EconomyEnglish
| मार्च 2025 तक, भारत में करोड़ सक्रिय घरेलू एल.पी.जी. उपभोक्ता हैं, जिनमें 0.33 करोड़ पी.एम.यू.वाई. लाभार्थी शामिल हैं।
A
32.94 crore
✓ Correct
B
38.94 crore
C
42.94 crore
D
22.94 crore

Solution

Correct answer: A. As of 1 March 2025, India had about 32.94 crore active domestic LPG consumers, including 10.33 crore PMUY (Ujjwala) beneficiaries.

B is incorrect: 38.94 crore overstates the active-consumer figure cited.

C is incorrect: 42.94 crore is too high.

D is incorrect: 22.94 crore understates the number of active LPG consumers.

💡 The Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) provides free LPG connections to women from poor households.
Q.No 29 EconomyEnglish
Healthy life expectancy in the Happiness Index is based on:
A
Number of hospitals
B
Expected years of healthy living
✓ Correct
C
Total fertility rate
D
Average years of schooling

Solution

Correct answer: B. In the World Happiness Report, 'healthy life expectancy' measures the expected number of years a person can live in good health.

A is incorrect: The number of hospitals is an input, not the health outcome measured.

C is incorrect: The total fertility rate is a demographic measure, not health expectancy.

D is incorrect: Average years of schooling is an education indicator, not a health measure.

💡 Healthy life expectancy is one of the six key variables used to compute happiness scores.
Q.No 34 EconomyEnglish
The Manmohan Singh Committee (1991) is known for:
A
Liberalization and structural adjustment
✓ Correct
B
Banking reforms
C
Agricultural reforms
D
Fiscal reforms

Solution

Correct answer: A. The reforms associated with Manmohan Singh as Finance Minister in 1991 are known for liberalization and structural adjustment - opening up the economy, reducing licensing and devaluing the rupee.

B is incorrect: Banking reforms were a part, but the headline identity of the 1991 reforms is liberalization/structural adjustment.

C is incorrect: Agricultural reforms were not the focus of the 1991 package.

D is incorrect: Fiscal reforms were included, but the defining theme was broad liberalization.

💡 The 1991 reforms (LPG - Liberalization, Privatization, Globalization) followed a severe balance-of-payments crisis.
Q.No 35 EconomyEnglish
The bank nationalization in 1969 was primarily aimed at:
A
Promoting exports
B
Controlling inflation
C
Reducing foreign investment
D
Extending credit to agriculture, small industries, and priority sectors
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The 1969 nationalization of 14 major banks was primarily aimed at extending credit to agriculture, small industries and other priority sectors, and spreading banking to rural areas.

A is incorrect: Promoting exports was not the chief purpose of bank nationalization.

B is incorrect: Controlling inflation is a monetary-policy goal, not the main aim here.

C is incorrect: Reducing foreign investment was not the objective.

💡 Bank nationalization greatly expanded rural bank branches and priority-sector lending in India.
Q.No 36 EconomyEnglish
Which economist gave the concept of comparative advantage?
A
Keynes
B
Adam Smith
C
Hicks
D
Ricardo
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The concept of comparative advantage in international trade was given by the economist David Ricardo.

A is incorrect: Keynes is known for macroeconomics and demand management.

B is incorrect: Adam Smith gave the idea of absolute advantage (and the 'invisible hand').

C is incorrect: Hicks contributed to general-equilibrium and welfare economics.

💡 Comparative advantage shows that countries gain from trade even if one is less efficient at producing everything.
Q.No 37 EconomyEnglish
Which of the following most accurately reflects the fundamental orientation of public sector industries in India?
A
They are primarily export-driven enterprises
B
They function with the exclusive motive of profit maximization
C
They are predominantly under foreign ownership and control
D
They prioritize wider socio-economic developmental objectives
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Public-sector industries in India are fundamentally oriented towards wider socio-economic development objectives - employment, balanced regional growth and self-reliance - rather than pure profit.

A is incorrect: They are not primarily export-driven.

B is incorrect: Profit maximization is not their exclusive motive.

C is incorrect: They are state-owned, not predominantly under foreign ownership.

💡 Public-sector undertakings were envisaged to occupy the 'commanding heights' of the economy after 1956.
Q.No 29 EconomyHindi
व्यापार सुगमता सूचकांक (६००8) में “बिजली प्राप्ति” संकेतक __ मापता है।
A
बिजली वाले घरों की संख्या
B
राष्ट्रीय बिजली उत्पादन
C
बिजली प्राप्त करने की लागत, समय और प्रक्रियाएँ
✓ Correct
D
ऊर्जा दक्षता

Solution

Correct answer: C. In the Ease of Doing Business framework, the 'Getting Electricity' indicator measures the cost, time and number of procedures required to obtain an electricity connection.

A is incorrect: The number of electrified households is not what this indicator measures.

B is incorrect: National electricity generation is a separate energy statistic.

D is incorrect: Energy efficiency is unrelated to this indicator.

💡 It also factors in the reliability of supply and transparency of tariffs.
Q.No 33 EconomyHindi
भारतीय नियोजन का जनक किसे माना जाता है?
A
दादाभाई नौरोजी
B
जवाहरलाल नेहरू
C
महालनोबिस
D
एम. विश्वेश्वरैया
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Sir M. Visvesvaraya is regarded as the father of Indian (economic) planning; his book 'Planned Economy for India' (1934) outlined a structured plan for development.

A is incorrect: Dadabhai Naoroji is known for the 'drain of wealth' theory.

B is incorrect: Jawaharlal Nehru championed planning and set up the Planning Commission but is not called its father.

C is incorrect: P.C. Mahalanobis designed the model for the Second Five-Year Plan.

💡 Visvesvaraya, a celebrated engineer, is honoured on Engineer's Day (15 September) in India.
Q.No 34 EconomyEnglish
औद्योगिक लाइसेंसिंग प्रणाली __ में शुरू की गई थी
A
1948
B
1951
✓ Correct
C
1956
D
1969

Solution

Correct answer: B. The industrial licensing system was introduced in 1951 through the Industries (Development and Regulation) Act.

A is incorrect: 1948 was the first Industrial Policy Resolution, not the licensing Act.

C is incorrect: 1956 was the second Industrial Policy Resolution.

D is incorrect: 1969 was the year of bank nationalization and the MRTP Act.

💡 This licensing regime (the 'Licence Raj') was largely dismantled by the 1991 reforms.
Q.No 36 EconomyHindi
राजकोषीय उत्तरदायित्व और बजट प्रबंधन (एफआरबीएम) अधिनियम का मुख्य उद्देश्य क्या है?
A
मुद्रा आपूर्ति को नियंत्रित करना
B
निर्यात का प्रबंधन करना
C
सहकारी बैंकिंग को बढ़ावा देना
D
राजकोषीय घाटे को कम करना
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The main objective of the Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management (FRBM) Act, 2003 is to reduce the fiscal deficit and ensure prudent, disciplined public finances.

A is incorrect: Controlling money supply is the job of the RBI's monetary policy.

B is incorrect: Managing exports is the role of trade policy.

C is incorrect: Promoting cooperative banking is unrelated to the FRBM Act.

💡 The FRBM Act sets targets for the government to limit borrowing and bring down the deficit over time.
Q.No 38 EconomyEnglish
आर्थिक सर्वेक्षण 2024-25 के अनुसार, सामाजिक सेवा व्यय ने वित्त वर्ष 202] और वित्त वर्ष 2025 के बीच की वार्षिक वृद्धि दर दर्ज की है।
A
2%
B
15%
✓ Correct
C
21%
D
33%

Solution

Correct answer: B. As per the Economic Survey 2024-25, social-service expenditure recorded an annual growth rate of about 15% between FY21 and FY25.

A is incorrect: 2% greatly understates the growth.

C is incorrect: 21% is higher than the figure reported.

D is incorrect: 33% overstates the growth rate.

💡 Social-service expenditure covers areas like education, health and social welfare.
Q.No 40 EconomyEnglish
2025 में, आई.एम.एफ. ने 2025 के लिए भारत की जी.डी.पी. वृद्धि दर लगभग रहने का अनुमान लगाया हैं।
A
4.4%
B
6.4%
✓ Correct
C
7.1%
D
8.5%

Solution

Correct answer: B. In 2025 the IMF projected India's GDP growth for 2025 at about 6.4%, keeping India among the fastest-growing major economies.

A is incorrect: 4.4% is far below the projected rate.

C is incorrect: 7.1% is higher than the IMF's projection.

D is incorrect: 8.5% overstates the projected growth.

💡 The IMF publishes its growth forecasts in the World Economic Outlook (WEO) reports.
Q.No 29 EconomyHindi
कौन सी प्रवृत्ति दर्शाती है कि 99 के सुधारों से “असमानता के साथ विकास” हुआ?
A
ग्रामीण गरीबी में शहरी गरीबी की तुलना में तेजी से कमी आई
B
कृषि रोजगार औद्योगिक रोजगार की तुलना में तेजी से बढ़ा
C
अनौपचारिक क्षेत्र में उल्लेखनीय गिरावट आई
D
शहरी सेवा क्षेत्र का तेजी से विस्तार हुआ जबकि कृषि स्थिर रही
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. After the 1991 reforms, the urban service sector expanded rapidly while agriculture stagnated, producing 'growth with inequality' between sectors and regions.

A is incorrect: Rural poverty did not fall faster than urban poverty in this pattern.

B is incorrect: Agricultural employment did not grow faster than industrial employment.

C is incorrect: The informal sector did not decline notably; much employment remained informal.

💡 Services now contribute over half of India's GDP, reflecting this services-led growth.
Q.No 32 EconomyHindi
99 के बाद के कौन से वित्तीय क्षेत्र सुधारों में शामिल थे?
A
निजी बैंकों का प्रवेश
B
पूंजी बाजारों को मजबूत करना
C
ब्याज दरों का विनियमन
D
ये सभी
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Post-1991 financial-sector reforms included the entry of private banks, strengthening of capital markets, and deregulation of interest rates - so all of these apply.

A is incorrect: Entry of private banks is correct but only one part of the answer.

B is incorrect: Strengthening capital markets is correct but not the whole answer.

C is incorrect: Deregulation of interest rates is correct but not the complete answer alone.

💡 SEBI was given statutory powers in 1992 to regulate and strengthen the capital markets.
Q.No 28 EconomyHindi
द्वितीय पंचवर्षीय योजना (956-6) मुख्य रूप से किस पर केंद्रित थी?
A
हस्तशिल्प के निर्यात को बढ़ावा देना
B
औद्योगीकरण और भारी उद्योग
✓ Correct
C
सेवा क्षेत्र का विस्तार
D
कृषि का पूर्ण निजीकरण

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Second Five-Year Plan (1956-61), based on the Mahalanobis model, focused mainly on industrialization and the building of heavy industries.

A is incorrect: Promoting handicraft exports was not the Second Plan's main focus.

C is incorrect: Expanding the service sector was not the priority of the Second Plan.

D is incorrect: It did not aim at full privatization of agriculture.

💡 The Second Plan set up public-sector steel plants at Bhilai, Rourkela and Durgapur.
Q.No 31 EconomyHindi
भारत में 950-980 के दशक की व्यापार नीति में __ पर जोर दिया गया।
A
पूर्ण उदारीकरण
B
आयात प्रतिस्थापन और संरक्षणवाद
✓ Correct
C
मुक्त व्यापार के माध्यम से निर्यात संवर्धन
D
विदेशी पोर्टफोलियो निवेश

Solution

Correct answer: B. India's trade policy from the 1950s to the 1980s emphasised import substitution and protectionism - encouraging domestic production behind high tariffs and import controls.

A is incorrect: Full liberalization came only with the 1991 reforms.

C is incorrect: Export promotion through free trade was not the dominant pre-1991 strategy.

D is incorrect: Foreign portfolio investment was not emphasised in that era.

💡 Import substitution aimed at self-reliance but is criticised for creating inefficient, sheltered industries.
Q.No 32 EconomyEnglish
भारतीय खाद्य निगम (#0८।) की स्थापना __ में हुई थी
A
1951
B
1965
✓ Correct
C
1968
D
1974

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Food Corporation of India (FCI) was established in 1965 to procure, store and distribute food grains and ensure food security.

A is incorrect: 1951 is incorrect.

C is incorrect: 1968 is not the founding year.

D is incorrect: 1974 is too late.

💡 The FCI manages the buffer stock and supports the Minimum Support Price and Public Distribution System.
Q.No 35 EconomyHindi
भारत की निर्यात वृद्धि को प्रोत्साहित करने के लिए 99 में कौन सा व्यापार-संबंधी नीति सुधार शुरू किया गया था?
A
आयात प्रतिस्थापन रणनीति
B
निर्यात-आयात नीति ढांचा
C
आर्थिक उदारीकरण नीति
✓ Correct
D
हरित क्रांति रणनीति

Solution

Correct answer: C. The economic liberalization policy launched in 1991 opened up trade and reduced controls, with the aim (among others) of boosting India's export growth.

A is incorrect: Import substitution was the older, pre-1991 strategy being abandoned.

B is incorrect: The Exim policy framework existed earlier; the 1991 landmark was broad liberalization.

D is incorrect: The Green Revolution was an agricultural strategy, not a trade-policy reform.

💡 The 1991 reforms slashed tariffs, dismantled licensing and devalued the rupee to spur exports.
Q.No 40 EconomyEnglish
2025 में, आई.एम.एफ. ने 2026 के लिए भारत की जी.डी.पी. वृद्धि दर लगभग रहने का अनुमान लगाया हैं।
A
6.4%
✓ Correct
B
7.1%
C
8.5%
D
7.9%

Solution

Correct answer: A. In 2025 the IMF projected India's GDP growth for 2026 at about 6.4%, indicating continued strong expansion.

B is incorrect: 7.1% is higher than the projected figure.

C is incorrect: 8.5% overstates the projection.

D is incorrect: 7.9% is also higher than projected.

💡 India has remained the fastest-growing major economy in recent IMF projections.
Q.No 38 EconomyHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
2025 में समुद्री विकास निधि के लिए आवंटित बजट राशि कितनी है?
A
₹5,000 करोड़
B
₹0,000 करोड़
C
₹25,000 करोड़
✓ Correct
D
₹50,000 करोड़

Solution

Correct answer: C. In 2025 the Maritime Development Fund was allocated about Rs 25,000 crore to support the shipping and maritime sector.

A is incorrect: Rs 5,000 crore understates the allocation.

B is incorrect: Rs 10,000 crore is lower than the figure announced.

D is incorrect: Rs 50,000 crore overstates the allocation.

💡 The fund aims to boost India's shipbuilding, ports and overall maritime ecosystem.
Q.No 31 EconomyEnglish
The Goods and Services Tax Council is chaired by:
A
Prime Minister
B
NITI Aayog CEO
C
RBI Governor
D
Finance Minister of India
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The Goods and Services Tax (GST) Council is chaired by the Union Finance Minister of India.

A is incorrect: The Prime Minister does not chair the GST Council.

B is incorrect: The NITI Aayog CEO is not the chairperson.

C is incorrect: The RBI Governor handles monetary policy, not the GST Council.

💡 State finance ministers are members of the GST Council, making it a federal body (Article 279A).
Q.No 39 EconomyEnglish
Which of the following is NOT the way to finance a budgetary deficit? I. Borrowings II. Taxation
A
Only I
B
Only II
C
Both I and II
D
Neither I nor II
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Both borrowings (I) and taxation (II) are legitimate ways by which a government finances its expenditure and bridges a budgetary gap, so neither is excluded - hence 'Neither I nor II'.

A is incorrect: 'Only I' wrongly excludes taxation as a source of finance.

B is incorrect: 'Only II' wrongly excludes borrowings.

C is incorrect: 'Both I and II' would mean both are NOT ways, which is incorrect.

💡 A government typically meets its spending through tax revenue, non-tax revenue and borrowings.
Q.No 25 EconomyHindi
निम्नलिखित में से कौन सी रिपोर्ट मानव विकास से संबंधित है?
A
वैश्विक जोखिम रिपोर्ट
B
मानव विकास रिपोर्ट
✓ Correct
C
विश्व आर्थिक परिदृश्य
D
वैश्विक प्रतिस्पर्धात्मकता रिपोर्ट

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Human Development Report is the publication directly concerned with human development; it introduced the Human Development Index.

A is incorrect: The Global Risks Report (WEF) deals with global risks.

C is incorrect: The World Economic Outlook (IMF) covers growth and economic prospects.

D is incorrect: The Global Competitiveness Report ranks economies on competitiveness.

💡 The Human Development Report has been published by the UNDP since 1990.
Q.No 26 EconomyHindi
वैश्विक ऊर्जा संक्रमण सूचकांक किसके द्वारा प्रकाशित किया जाता है?
A
अंतर्राष्ट्रीय ऊर्जा एजेंसी
B
विश्व बैंक
C
विश्व आर्थिक मंच
✓ Correct
D
पेट्रोलियम निर्यातक देशों का संगठन

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Energy Transition Index (Global Energy Transition Index) is published by the World Economic Forum (WEF).

A is incorrect: The International Energy Agency publishes other energy reports, not this index.

B is incorrect: The World Bank does not publish the Energy Transition Index.

D is incorrect: OPEC is an oil producers' group, not the publisher.

💡 The index measures how ready countries are to shift to clean, sustainable energy systems.
Q.No 30 EconomyHindi
भारतीय जीवन बीमा निगम के राष्ट्रीयकरण का क्या उद्देश्य था?
A
ग्रामीण रोज़गार को बढ़ावा देना
B
कृषि का विकास करना
C
विदेशी व्यापार को विनियमित करना
D
नियोजित विकास के लिए बचत जुटाना
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. A major aim of nationalising the Life Insurance Corporation (LIC) in 1956 was to mobilise people's savings to fund the country's planned development.

A is incorrect: Promoting rural employment was not the primary aim.

B is incorrect: Developing agriculture was not the direct purpose.

C is incorrect: Regulating foreign trade was unrelated to LIC's nationalization.

💡 LIC was created in 1956 by merging many private insurers into a single state-owned corporation.
Q.No 39 EconomyHindi
भारतीय बजट के संदर्भ में, निम्नलिखित में से किसे राजस्व व्यय में शामिल किया जाता है?
A
उपदान
✓ Correct
B
लड़ाकू विमानों के अधिग्रहण पर व्यय
C
नौसैनिक जहाज के अधिग्रहण पर व्यय
D
एक्सप्रेसवे निर्माण पर व्यय

Solution

Correct answer: A. In the Indian budget, a subsidy is classified under revenue expenditure, since it does not create an asset and is a recurring transfer.

B is incorrect: Acquiring fighter aircraft is capital expenditure (it creates an asset).

C is incorrect: Acquiring a naval ship is capital expenditure.

D is incorrect: Expressway construction is capital expenditure (asset creation).

💡 Revenue expenditure is for the normal running of government and does not create assets; capital expenditure builds or buys assets.
Q.No 30 EconomyHindi
स्वतंत्र भारत में प्रथम इस्पात संयंत्र (भिलाई, राउरकेला और दुर्गापुर) का उद्देश्य क्या था?
A
डेयरी उत्पादन
B
निर्यात संवर्धन
C
इस्पात और भारी उद्योग में आत्मनिर्भरता
✓ Correct
D
ग्रामीण रोज़गार

Solution

Correct answer: C. The first steel plants of independent India (Bhilai, Rourkela and Durgapur) aimed to achieve self-reliance in steel and heavy industry.

A is incorrect: They had nothing to do with dairy production.

B is incorrect: They were for domestic capacity, not primarily export promotion.

D is incorrect: Rural employment was not their main objective.

💡 These plants were set up during the Second Five-Year Plan with foreign technical collaboration (USSR, Germany, UK).
Q.No 38 EconomyHindi
एक निश्चित विनिमय दर प्रणाली में, जब किसी सरकारी कार्रवाई द्वारा विनिमय दर बढ़ाती हैं, तो इसे कहा जाता है, जिससे घरेलू मुद्रा सस्ती हो जाती हैं।
A
मूल्यहास
B
मूल्यवृद्धि
C
अवमूल्यन
✓ Correct
D
पुर्गमूल्यन

Solution

Correct answer: C. Under a fixed exchange-rate system, when the government officially raises the exchange rate so that the domestic currency becomes cheaper, it is called devaluation.

A is incorrect: Depreciation is a fall in a currency's value under a floating system, not a deliberate official act under a fixed system.

B is incorrect: Appreciation is a rise in currency value.

D is incorrect: Revaluation is an official increase in the currency's value (the opposite of devaluation).

💡 India devalued the rupee in 1966 and again sharply in 1991.
Q.No 24 EconomyEnglish
Freedom to make life choices in the Happiness Index refers to:
A
Freedom of media
B
People’s perception of having control over life decisions
✓ Correct
C
Economic liberalization
D
Political democracy

Solution

Correct answer: B. In the Happiness Index, 'freedom to make life choices' refers to people's perception that they have the freedom/control to make their own life decisions.

A is incorrect: It is not about freedom of the media.

C is incorrect: It is not about economic liberalization.

D is incorrect: It is broader than formal political democracy; it is about personal life choices.

💡 It is one of the six factors used in the World Happiness Report.
Q.No 26 EconomyEnglish
By 1990, India’s external debt had become:
A
Zero
B
Negligible
C
Manageable
D
Very high, leading to crisis
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. By 1990, India's external debt had become very high, contributing to the 1991 balance-of-payments crisis.

A is incorrect: The debt was not zero.

B is incorrect: It was far from negligible.

C is incorrect: It was not manageable; it precipitated a crisis.

💡 In 1991 India had to pledge gold reserves and seek IMF assistance, triggering economic reforms.
Q.No 37 EconomyEnglish
In the context of money supply in India, which of the following is NOT the Quantitative tool of Reserve bank of India?
A
Cash Reserve Ratio
B
Bank rate
C
Open Market Operations
D
Moral suasion
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Moral suasion is a qualitative (selective) tool of the RBI, not a quantitative one - so it is NOT a quantitative tool.

A is incorrect: The Cash Reserve Ratio is a quantitative tool.

B is incorrect: The Bank Rate is a quantitative tool.

C is incorrect: Open Market Operations are a quantitative tool.

💡 Quantitative tools affect the overall money supply; qualitative tools (like moral suasion) target specific sectors or behaviour.
Q.No 24 EconomyHindi
मानव विकास सूचकांक (१०) में, शिक्षा सूचकांक को __ का उपयोग करके मापा जाता है।
A
स्कूली शिक्षा के औसत वर्ष और स्कूली शिक्षा के अपेक्षित वर्ष
✓ Correct
B
नामांकन अनुपात और ड्रॉपआउट अनुपात
C
साक्षरता दर और स्कूल नामांकन अनुपात
D
वयस्क साक्षरता दर और शिक्षक-छात्र अनुपात

Solution

Correct answer: A. In the Human Development Index, the education dimension is measured using mean years of schooling and expected years of schooling.

B is incorrect: Enrolment and dropout ratios are not the HDI education measures.

C is incorrect: Literacy rate and school enrolment were used in the older HDI, but the current measure uses mean and expected years of schooling.

D is incorrect: Adult literacy and teacher-student ratio are not the HDI education indicators.

💡 The HDI combines education, income (GNI per capita) and life expectancy.
Q.No 27 EconomyHindi
बाजार अर्थव्यवस्था का दूसरा नाम क्या है?
A
साम्यवाद
B
सामंतवाद
C
पूँजीवाद
✓ Correct
D
समाजवाद

Solution

Correct answer: C. A market economy is also known as capitalism, where production and prices are determined mainly by private decisions and market forces.

A is incorrect: Communism is a system of collective/state ownership.

B is incorrect: Feudalism is a medieval land-based hierarchy.

D is incorrect: Socialism emphasises state/collective ownership and planning.

💡 India follows a 'mixed economy', combining elements of capitalism and socialism.
Q.No 36 EconomyHindi
भारत में मुद्रा आपूर्ति के संदर्भ में, का तात्पर्य खुले बाजार में भारत सरकार द्वारा जारी बंधपत्र की खरीद और बिक्री से है।
A
नैतिक दबाव
B
सीमा अनिवार्यता
C
खुले बाज़ार की क्रियाएँ
✓ Correct
D
ऋणों का सदाबहारीकरण

Solution

Correct answer: C. Open Market Operations refer to the buying and selling of government bonds/securities by the RBI in the open market to control the money supply.

A is incorrect: Moral suasion is persuasion, not bond buying/selling.

B is incorrect: 'Margin requirement' relates to loan collateral, not open-market bond trades.

D is incorrect: 'Evergreening of loans' refers to extending fresh loans to hide bad debt.

💡 Selling bonds reduces money supply; buying bonds injects liquidity into the economy.
Q.No 23 EconomyHindi
भारतीय जनगणना में कार्यशील जनसंख्या को निम्नलिखित तीन श्रेणियों में से किसमें वर्गीकृत किया गया है?
A
मुख्य श्रमिक, सीमांत श्रमिक और गैर-श्रमिक
✓ Correct
B
कृषि, औद्योगिक और सेवा श्रमिक
C
संगठित, असंगठित, बेरोजगार
D
ग्रामीण, शहरी और प्रवासी श्रमिक

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Indian Census classifies the working population into three categories: main workers, marginal workers and non-workers.

B is incorrect: Agricultural/industrial/service is a sectoral split, not the census worker classification.

C is incorrect: Organised/unorganised/unemployed is not the census categorisation.

D is incorrect: Rural/urban/migrant is not the worker classification used.

💡 A 'main worker' works for at least 183 days (6 months) in a year; below that one is a 'marginal worker'.
Q.No 25 EconomyHindi
भारत में, हरित क्रांति में के उच्च पैदावार वाली किस्मों के बीजों का उपयोग किया गया था।
A
चावल और गेहूं
✓ Correct
B
गन्ना और चाय
C
ज्वार और बाजरा
D
सरसों और रागी

Solution

Correct answer: A. India's Green Revolution used high-yielding variety (HYV) seeds mainly of rice and wheat, dramatically increasing food-grain output.

B is incorrect: Sugarcane and tea were not the focus of HYV seeds in the Green Revolution.

C is incorrect: Jowar and bajra (millets) were not the main HYV crops then.

D is incorrect: Mustard and ragi were not the Green Revolution's lead crops.

💡 M.S. Swaminathan is regarded as the father of India's Green Revolution.
Q.No 34 EconomyHindi
भारतीय अर्थव्यवस्था के संदर्भ में, वैयक्तिक प्रयोज्य आय (पी.डी.आई.) के संदर्भ में निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा कथन सही है?
A
पी.डी.आई. न वैयक्तिक आय - वैयक्तिक कर अदायगी + गैरकर अदायगी
B
पी.डी.आई. वैयक्तिक आय - व्यक्तिगत कर अदायगी - गैरकर अदायगी
✓ Correct
C
पी.डी.आई. + राष्ट्रीय आय - वैयक्तिक कर अदायगी + गैरकर अदायगी
D
पी.डी.आई. - राष्ट्रीय आय - वैयक्तिक कर अदायगी - गैरकर अदायगी

Solution

Correct answer: B. Personal Disposable Income (PDI) = Personal Income minus personal (direct) tax payments minus non-tax payments (such as fees/fines to government).

A is incorrect: It subtracts non-tax payments too, not adds them.

C is incorrect: PDI starts from personal income, not national income.

D is incorrect: PDI is not national income minus those items.

💡 Disposable income is what households can actually spend or save after compulsory payments.
Q.No 39 EconomyHindi
एनर्जी ट्रांजीशन इंडेक्स 2025 में कौन सा देश प्रथम स्थान पर है?
A
स्वीडन
✓ Correct
B
फिनलैंड
C
डेनमार्क
D
यु.एस.ए.

Solution

Correct answer: A. Sweden ranked first in the Energy Transition Index 2025, reflecting its progress towards clean and secure energy.

B is incorrect: Finland ranked high but not first.

C is incorrect: Denmark ranked among the leaders but not first.

D is incorrect: The USA did not top the index.

💡 The Energy Transition Index is published by the World Economic Forum.
Q.No 30 EconomyEnglish
सी.ओ.पी.-26 (कॉन्फ्रेंस ऑफ पार्टीज) में किए गए संकल्प के अनुसार, भारत में तक 500 गीगावाट गैर-जीवाश्म ईंधन बिजली क्षमता के लक्ष्य तक पहुंचने के लिए प्रयास किए जा रहे हैं।
A
2027
B
2030
✓ Correct
C
2035
D
2040

Solution

Correct answer: B. At COP-26 India pledged to reach 500 GW of non-fossil-fuel electricity capacity by 2030.

A is incorrect: 2027 is not the target year.

C is incorrect: 2035 is not the pledged year.

D is incorrect: 2040 is the carbon-related target for some pledges, but the 500 GW goal is 2030.

💡 India also pledged at COP-26 to achieve net-zero emissions by 2070.
Q.No 24 EconomyHindi
99 के सुधारों को कभी-कभी “बेरोजगारी विहीन वृद्धि” से क्यों जोड़ा जाता है?
A
कृषि में रोजगार का बढ़ता हिस्सा
B
आनुपातिक रोजगार विस्तार के बिना उच्च जीडीपी वृद्धि
✓ Correct
C
शिक्षित बेरोजगारी में तेजी से कमी आई
D
ग्रामीण क्षेत्रों ने अधिक श्रम को अवशोषित किया

Solution

Correct answer: B. The 1991 reforms are sometimes called 'jobless growth' because they produced high GDP growth without a proportional expansion of employment.

A is incorrect: Agricultural employment did not rise sharply.

C is incorrect: Educated unemployment did not fall rapidly.

D is incorrect: Rural areas did not absorb much more labour.

💡 Services-led growth created relatively fewer jobs than the size of the workforce required.
Q.No 26 EconomyHindi
980 की नई औद्योगिक नीति का क्या उद्देश्य था?
A
लघु-स्तरीय आधुनिकीकरण और प्रौद्योगिकी समावेशन को प्रोत्साहित करें
✓ Correct
B
निजी उद्योगों को बंद करें
C
सार्वजनिक क्षेत्र पर सख्त नियंत्रण बनाए रखें
D
प्रमुख उद्योगों का राष्ट्रीयकरण करें

Solution

Correct answer: A. The New Industrial Policy of 1980 aimed to encourage modernization of small-scale industry and the absorption of improved technology.

B is incorrect: It did not aim to close private industries.

C is incorrect: It did not aim at stricter control over the public sector.

D is incorrect: It did not aim to nationalize major industries.

💡 The 1980 policy laid groundwork for the gradual liberalization that accelerated in 1991.
Q.No 27 EconomyHindi
मिश्रित अर्थव्यवस्था में निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा कार्य सरकार का नहीं है?
A
सभी वस्तुओं की कीमतें निर्धारित करना
✓ Correct
B
बाज़ारों का विनियमन
C
सार्वजनिक वस्तुओं का प्रावधान
D
आय का पुनर्वितरण

Solution

Correct answer: A. In a mixed economy, setting the prices of all goods is NOT a function of the government - prices are largely determined by market forces.

B is incorrect: Regulating markets is a government function.

C is incorrect: Providing public goods is a government function.

D is incorrect: Redistributing income (through taxes/transfers) is a government function.

💡 A mixed economy combines private markets with government intervention, as in India.
Q.No 24 EconomyOdia
ଭାରତୀୟ ଶିଳ୍ପ ଉପରେ ଉଦାରୀକରଣ ଏବଂ ଜଗତୀକରଣର କ'ଣ ସକାରାମୂକ ପ୍ରଭାବ ପଡ଼ିଥିଲା?
A
ପ୍ରତିଯୋଗିତା ଯୋଗୁଁ ଦକ୍ଷତା ହ୍ରାସ
B
ଉନ୍ନତ ପ୍ରଯୁକ୍ତିବିଦ୍ୟା ଏବଂ ବୃହତ ବଜାରରେ ପ୍ରବେଶ
✓ Correct
C
ଭାରତୀୟ ଫାର୍ମଗୁଡ଼ିକର ବଦ୍ଧିତ ଏକାକୀତା
D
ରାଜ୍ୟକୁ ଭିତ୍ତିଭୂମିରୁ ସମ୍ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ପ୍ରତ୍ୟାହାର କରିବା

Solution

Correct answer: B. A positive effect of liberalization and globalization on Indian industry was access to advanced technology and entry into larger (global) markets.

A is incorrect: Competition raised efficiency rather than reducing it.

C is incorrect: It increased competition for Indian firms, not their monopoly.

D is incorrect: It did not mean the state fully withdrew from infrastructure.

💡 Post-1991 liberalization integrated India more deeply with the world economy.
Q.No 25 EconomyOdia
1950-1980 ମଧ୍ୟରେ କେଉଁ ଶିଳକୁ ଅର୍ଥନୀତିର " କମାଣ୍ଡିଂ ହାଇଟୁ" ଭାବରେ ବିବେଚନା କରାଯାଉଥିଲା?
A
ବୟନ
B
ହସ୍ତକଳା
C
କୃଷି
D
ଭାରୀ ଶିଳ୍ପ, ଇସ୍ପାତ, ଶକ୍ତି
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Between 1950 and 1980, heavy industry, steel and power were considered the 'commanding heights' of the economy, reserved largely for the public sector.

A is incorrect: Textiles were not the 'commanding heights'.

B is incorrect: Handicrafts were not the commanding heights.

C is incorrect: Agriculture, though vital, was not termed the commanding heights.

💡 The 'commanding heights' idea came from the 1956 Industrial Policy Resolution.
Q.No 26 EconomyOdia
ପ୍ରଯୁକ୍ତିବିଦ୍ୟା ପ୍ରଗତି ଯୋଗୁଁ କେଉଁ ପ୍ରକାରର ବେକାରୀ ସୃଷ୍ଟି ହୁଏ?
A
ରତୁକାଳୀନ
B
ଛଦ୍ମବେଶୀ
C
ଘର୍ଷଣାତ୍ମକ
D
ଗଠନମୂଳକ
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Technological progress that replaces workers with machines creates structural unemployment.

A is incorrect: Seasonal unemployment is linked to seasons (e.g. farming), not technology.

B is incorrect: Disguised unemployment is surplus labour with near-zero productivity (common in agriculture).

C is incorrect: Frictional unemployment is the short-term gap while changing jobs.

💡 Structural unemployment arises from a mismatch between workers' skills and available jobs.
Q.No 32 EconomyEnglish
ଭାରତରେ ମୁଖ୍ୟ ଶ୍ରମିକ ହେଉଛନ୍ତି ଜଣେ ବ୍ୟକ୍ତି ଯିଏ ବର୍ଷରେ ଅତି କମରେ ଦିନ କାମ କରନ୍ତି ।
A
83
B
90
C
100
D
183
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. In India, a 'main worker' is a person who works for at least 183 days (about six months) in a year.

A is incorrect: 83 days is too few.

B is incorrect: 90 days is below the main-worker threshold.

C is incorrect: 100 days corresponds to MGNREGA's guarantee, not the main-worker definition.

💡 Those who work for less than 183 days are classified as 'marginal workers' in the Census.
Q.No 35 EconomyOdia
ରାସାୟନିକ ଭିତ୍ତିକ ଶିଳ୍ପ ସମ୍ବନ୍ଧରେ ନିମୂଲିଖିତ ମଧ୍ୟରୁ କେଉଁଟି ଠିକ ଅଟେ? 1. ସିନ୍ଥେଟିକ ଫାଇବର ରାସାୟନିକ ଭିତ୍ତିକ ଶିଳ୍ୁର ଏକ ଉଦାହରଣ ଅଟେ । ` - ର 11. ଲବଣ, ସଲଫର ଏବଂ ପଟାସ ଉଦ୍ୟୋଗଗୁଡ଼ିକ ମଧ୍ୟ ପ୍ରାକୃତିକ ଖଣିଜ ପଦାଥର ବ୍ୟବହାର କରିଥାନ୍ତି ।
A
Only I
B
Only II
C
Both I and II
✓ Correct
D
Neither I nor II

Solution

Correct answer: C. Both statements are correct: synthetic fibre (I) is an example of a chemical-based industry, and salt, sulphur and potash industries (II) also use natural minerals - so 'Both I and II' is right.

A is incorrect: 'Only I' omits the correct second statement.

B is incorrect: 'Only II' omits the correct first statement.

D is incorrect: 'Neither' is wrong since both statements are correct.

💡 Chemical industries are classified into organic and inorganic branches.
Q.No 24 EconomyHindi
950 और 990 के बीच भारतीय कृषि के सामने निम्नलिखित में से कौन सी चुनौती नहीं थी?
A
खंडित भूमि जोत
B
मानसून वर्षा पर निर्भरता
C
उर्वरकों और सिंचाई का कम उपयोग
D
उच्च ग्रामीण साक्षरता दर से श्रम आपूर्ति में कमी
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. High rural literacy reducing the labour supply was NOT a challenge facing Indian agriculture between 1950 and 1990 - in fact rural literacy was low and labour was abundant.

A is incorrect: Fragmented landholdings were a genuine challenge.

B is incorrect: Dependence on monsoon rainfall was a real challenge.

C is incorrect: Low use of fertilizers and irrigation was a genuine problem.

💡 The Green Revolution addressed some of these challenges through HYV seeds, fertilizers and irrigation.
Q.No 25 EconomyHindi
भारत-चीन युद्ध के कारण कौन-सी योजना पूरी होने से एक वर्ष पहले ही समाप्त कर दी गई थी?
A
पहली योजना
B
दूसरी योजना
C
तीसरी योजना
✓ Correct
D
चौथी योजना

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Third Five-Year Plan was terminated a year early because of the disruption caused by the 1962 India-China war (and later wars/droughts).

A is incorrect: The First Plan was completed.

B is incorrect: The Second Plan was completed.

D is incorrect: The Fourth Plan came after a 'plan holiday'.

💡 After the Third Plan, India had a three-year 'plan holiday' (1966-69).
Q.No 27 EconomyHindi
स्थिर मूल्यों पर मापी गई राष्ट्रीय आय को क्या कहते हैं?
A
वास्तविक आय
✓ Correct
B
नाममात्र की आय
C
व्यय योग्य आय
D
शुद्ध कारक आय

Solution

Correct answer: A. National income measured at constant (base-year) prices is called real income (real GDP/national income), as it removes the effect of price changes.

B is incorrect: Nominal income is measured at current prices.

C is incorrect: Disposable income is income after taxes.

D is incorrect: Net factor income is a separate concept.

💡 Real income reflects the actual change in the volume of goods and services produced.
Q.No 39 EconomyHindi
ग्लोबल बिजनेस कॉम्प्लेक्सिटी इंडेक्स 2025 निम्नलिखित में से किसके द्वारा प्रकाशित किया गया था?
A
टी.एम.एफ. ग्रुप
✓ Correct
B
अंतर्राष्ट्रीय मुद्रा कोष
C
विश्व व्यापार संगठन
D
इकोनॉमिस्ट इंटेलिजेंस यूनिट

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Global Business Complexity Index 2025 was published by the TMF Group.

B is incorrect: The IMF did not publish it.

C is incorrect: The WTO did not publish it.

D is incorrect: The Economist Intelligence Unit did not publish this index.

💡 The index ranks jurisdictions by how complex it is to do business there.
Q.No 39 EconomyEnglish
ग्लोबल बिजनेस कॉम्प्लेक्सिटी इंडेक्स 2025 में भारत की रैंकिंग कया है?
A
71
B
33
C
11
D
18
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. India was ranked 18th in the Global Business Complexity Index 2025.

A is incorrect: 71 is incorrect.

B is incorrect: 33 is not India's rank.

C is incorrect: 11 is incorrect.

💡 A higher rank in this index means doing business is more complex in that jurisdiction.
Q.No 22 EconomyHindi
जीएसटी से पहले भारतीय राज्यों द्वारा मूल्य वर्धित कर (वैट) लागू करने का मुख्य उद्देश्य क्या था?
A
सभी प्रत्यक्ष करों को एकल कर से प्रतिस्थापित करना
B
अप्रत्यक्ष कराधान के व्यापक प्रभाव को न्यूनतम करना
✓ Correct
C
अधिक कर व्यय के माध्यम से राजकोषीय घाटे में वृद्धि
D
टैरिफ बढ़ाकर घरेलू उत्पादकों की रक्षा करना

Solution

Correct answer: B. Before GST, states introduced Value Added Tax (VAT) mainly to minimise the cascading ('tax on tax') effect of indirect taxation.

A is incorrect: VAT replaced sales tax (an indirect tax), not all direct taxes.

C is incorrect: It aimed to improve, not increase, fiscal deficits.

D is incorrect: It was not designed to raise tariffs for protection.

💡 GST (2017) further unified indirect taxes and removed cascading across the country.
Q.No 24 EconomyHindi
पांचवीं पंचवर्षीय योजना (974-79) में किस पर जोर दिया गया?
A
निर्यात-आधारित विकास
B
गरीबी उन्मूलन ("गरीबी हटाओ")
✓ Correct
C
बैंकिंग सुधार
D
भारी उद्योग

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Fifth Five-Year Plan (1974-79) emphasised poverty removal ('Garibi Hatao') and self-reliance.

A is incorrect: Export-led growth was not the Fifth Plan's focus.

C is incorrect: Banking reforms were not its theme.

D is incorrect: Heavy industry was the focus of the Second Plan.

💡 The Fifth Plan was cut short and the Rolling Plan concept was introduced afterwards.
Q.No 25 EconomyHindi
प्रच्छन्न बेरोजगारी या छिपी बेरोजगारी सामान्यतः: कहाँ पाई जाती है?
A
कृषि क्षेत्र
✓ Correct
B
आईटी क्षेत्र
C
औद्योगिक क्षेत्र
D
सेवा क्षेत्र

Solution

Correct answer: A. Disguised (hidden) unemployment is generally found in the agricultural sector, where more people work on a piece of land than are actually needed.

B is incorrect: The IT sector does not typically have disguised unemployment.

C is incorrect: The industrial sector is less prone to it.

D is incorrect: The service sector is less characterised by disguised unemployment.

💡 In disguised unemployment, removing some workers would not reduce total output.
Q.No 40 EconomyHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
विश्व बैंक के लॉजिस्टिक्स प्रदर्शन सूचकांक 2023 के अनुसार, भारत अंतर्राष्ट्रीय शिपमेंट श्रेणी में वैश्विक रैंकिंग में रैंक पर पहुंच गया है।
A
22nd
✓ Correct
B
12th
C
44th
D
31st

Solution

Correct answer: A. Per the World Bank's Logistics Performance Index 2023, India climbed to 22nd in the 'international shipments' category.

B is incorrect: 12th is not India's rank in that category.

C is incorrect: 44th is incorrect.

D is incorrect: 31st is not the rank.

💡 India's overall LPI 2023 rank improved to 38th, reflecting better logistics infrastructure.
Q.No 26 EconomyEnglish
निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा 978 में विमुद्रीकृत नोटों के एक मूल्यवर्ग नहीं है?
A
500
✓ Correct
B
1000
C
5000
D
10000

Solution

Correct answer: A. The 1978 demonetisation withdrew the Rs 1,000, Rs 5,000 and Rs 10,000 notes; the Rs 500 note was NOT among them.

B is incorrect: The Rs 1,000 note was demonetised in 1978.

C is incorrect: The Rs 5,000 note was demonetised in 1978.

D is incorrect: The Rs 10,000 note was demonetised in 1978.

💡 The 1978 demonetisation targeted high-value notes to curb black money.
Q.No 32 EconomyHindi
निम्नलिखित में से किसे वैधानिक पत्र माना जाता है?
A
Debit card
B
Currency note
✓ Correct
C
Cheque
D
Credit card

Solution

Correct answer: B. A currency note is legal tender (legal money) that must be accepted in settlement of debts.

A is incorrect: A debit card is a payment instrument, not legal tender.

C is incorrect: A cheque is a negotiable instrument, not legal tender.

D is incorrect: A credit card is a payment instrument, not legal tender.

💡 In India, currency notes are issued by the RBI (except the Re 1 note, issued by the Government).
Q.No 36 EconomyHindi
2020 में सार्वजनिक क्षेत्र के बैंकों में मेगा विलय में, निम्नलिखित में से किन बैंकों का भारतीय बैंक में विलय कर दिया गया था?
A
ओरिएंटल बैंक ऑफ कॉमर्स और यूनाइटेड बैंक ऑफ इंडिया
B
सिंडिकेट बैंक
C
आंध्रा बैंक और कॉर्पोरेशन बैंक
D
इलाहाबाद बैंक
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. In the 2020 mega-merger of public-sector banks, Allahabad Bank was merged into Indian Bank.

A is incorrect: Oriental Bank of Commerce and United Bank of India merged into Punjab National Bank.

B is incorrect: Syndicate Bank merged into Canara Bank.

C is incorrect: Andhra Bank and Corporation Bank merged into Union Bank of India.

💡 The 2020 consolidation reduced the number of public-sector banks to 12.
Q.No 26 EconomyEnglish
न ही सरकार ने बीमा क्षेत्र में सुधारों के लिए सिफारिशें प्रस्तावित करने हेतु आर.बी.आई. के पूर्व गवर्नर आर.एन. मल्होत्रा की अध्यक्षता में एक समिति गठित की थी।
A
1983
B
1963
C
1993
✓ Correct
D
1999

Solution

Correct answer: C. The government set up the Malhotra Committee (chaired by former RBI Governor R.N. Malhotra) in 1993 to recommend insurance-sector reforms.

A is incorrect: 1983 is incorrect.

B is incorrect: 1963 is incorrect.

D is incorrect: 1999 was when the IRDA Act was passed, following the committee's recommendations.

💡 The Malhotra Committee's report led to the opening up of the insurance sector and the creation of IRDA.
Q.No 32 EconomyHindi
जब कर संग्रह आवश्यक व्यय से अधिक हो जाता हैं, तो बजट को कहा जाता हैं।
A
घाटे का बजट
B
अधिक्य बजट
✓ Correct
C
संतुलित बजट
D
शून्य बजट

Solution

Correct answer: B. When tax collection exceeds necessary expenditure, the budget is called a surplus budget.

A is incorrect: A deficit budget is when expenditure exceeds revenue.

C is incorrect: A balanced budget is when revenue equals expenditure.

D is incorrect: A zero-based budget is a budgeting method, not this situation.

💡 Governments more commonly run deficit budgets to fund development spending.
Q.No 36 EconomyEnglish
|993 में विश्व सेवा निर्यात में भारत के सेवा निर्यात का हिस्सा कितना था?
A
0.5%
✓ Correct
B
5%
C
8%
D
12%

Solution

Correct answer: A. In 1993, India's share of world service exports was only about 0.5%.

B is incorrect: 5% overstates India's 1993 share.

C is incorrect: 8% is far too high for that period.

D is incorrect: 12% is much too high.

💡 India's services exports (especially IT) grew dramatically after the 1991 reforms.
Q.No 39 EconomyHindi
निम्नलिखित में से कौन भारत में "रेजिडेक्स" सूचकांक से संबंधित है?
A
शेयर बाजार का प्रदर्शन
B
औद्योगिक उत्पादन
C
कृषि वस्तु
D
आवास मूल्य
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. In India, the 'RESIDEX' index relates to housing prices (residential property price trends).

A is incorrect: It does not track stock-market performance.

B is incorrect: It does not track industrial production.

C is incorrect: It does not track agricultural commodities.

💡 NHB RESIDEX is India's official housing price index, published by the National Housing Bank.
Q.No 32 EconomyEnglish
भारत में लिंगगत बजट कब शुरू किया गया था?
A
1991-1992
B
1997-1998
C
2005-2006
✓ Correct
D
2020-2021

Solution

Correct answer: C. Gender budgeting was introduced in India in 2005-2006.

A is incorrect: 1991-92 was the year of economic reforms, not gender budgeting.

B is incorrect: 1997-98 is incorrect.

D is incorrect: 2020-21 is much later.

💡 Gender budgeting analyses the budget for its impact on women and allocates funds accordingly.
Q.No 25 EconomyHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
भारत की दसवीं पंचवर्षीय योजना का लक्ष्य निम्नलिखित में से क्या था? [. साक्षरता में सुधार। मी 7. शिशु एवं मातृ मृत्यु दर में कमी ।
A
Only I
B
Only II
C
Both I and II
✓ Correct
D
Neither I nor II

Solution

Correct answer: C. India's Tenth Five-Year Plan aimed at both improving literacy (I) and reducing infant and maternal mortality (II), so 'Both I and II' is correct.

A is incorrect: 'Only I' is incomplete.

B is incorrect: 'Only II' is incomplete.

D is incorrect: 'Neither' is wrong since both were goals.

💡 The Tenth Plan (2002-07) set monitorable human-development targets.
Q.No 32 EconomyHindi
बजट के संदर्भ में, निम्नलिखित में से किसमें सरकार की संपत्तियां तथा देनदारियां शामिल हैं?
A
राजस्व खाता
B
पूँजी खाता
✓ Correct
C
निलंब खाता
D
विदेशी मुद्रा खाता

Solution

Correct answer: B. In the government budget, the capital account includes the government's assets and liabilities.

A is incorrect: The revenue account covers recurring income and expenditure.

C is incorrect: A suspense account is a temporary holding account.

D is incorrect: A foreign-exchange account is not the budget head for assets/liabilities.

💡 Capital expenditure creates assets (or reduces liabilities), unlike revenue expenditure.
Q.No 36 EconomyHindi
सार्वजनिक क्षेत्र के बैंकों में मेगा विलय के संदर्भ में, आंध्रा बैंक को में समामेलित किया गया था।
A
यूनियन बैंक ऑफ इंडिया
✓ Correct
B
इंडियन बैंक
C
पंजाब नेशनल बैंक
D
केनरा बैंक

Solution

Correct answer: A. In the 2020 mega-merger of public-sector banks, Andhra Bank was amalgamated into Union Bank of India.

B is incorrect: It was not merged into Indian Bank.

C is incorrect: It was not merged into Punjab National Bank.

D is incorrect: It was not merged into Canara Bank.

💡 Corporation Bank was also merged into Union Bank of India in the same exercise.
Q.No 39 EconomyEnglish
एनर्जी ट्रांजीशन इंडेक्स 2025 में चीन का स्थान क्या है?
A
12
✓ Correct
B
22
C
33
D
41

Solution

Correct answer: A. China was ranked 12th in the Energy Transition Index 2025.

B is incorrect: 22 is not China's rank.

C is incorrect: 33 is incorrect.

D is incorrect: 41 is incorrect.

💡 The Energy Transition Index is published by the World Economic Forum.
Q.No 40 EconomyHindi
राष्ट्रीय बहुआयामी गरीबी सूचकांक (एम.पी.आई.) निम्नलिखित में से किसके द्वारा जारी किया जाता है?
A
नीति आयोग
✓ Correct
B
आई.डी.एस.ए.
C
एन.एच.आर.सी.
D
आई.आई.बी.

Solution

Correct answer: A. The National Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) is released by NITI Aayog (with UNDP and OPHI support).

B is incorrect: The IDSA is a defence think-tank.

C is incorrect: The NHRC deals with human rights.

D is incorrect: The 'IIB' does not publish the MPI.

💡 The MPI measures poverty across health, education and standard-of-living dimensions.
Q.No 40 EconomyHindi
निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा सूचकांक वर्ल्ड इकॉनोमिक फोरम द्वारा प्रकाशित नहीं किया जाता है?
A
ग्लोबल जेंडर गैप इंडेक्स
B
एनर्जी ट्रांजिशन इंडेक्स
C
ग्लोबल कंपटीटिवनेस इंडेक्स
D
ह्यूमन डेवलपमेंट इंडेक्स
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The Human Development Index is published by the UNDP, NOT by the World Economic Forum.

A is incorrect: The Global Gender Gap Index is published by the WEF.

B is incorrect: The Energy Transition Index is published by the WEF.

C is incorrect: The Global Competitiveness Index is published by the WEF.

💡 The WEF publishes several indices, but the HDI is a UNDP publication.
Q.No 34 EconomyHindi
मरकोसुर निम्नलिखित में से किस महाद्वीप से संबंधित एक व्यापारिक समूह है?
A
एशिया
B
यूरोप
C
अफ्रीका
D
दक्षिण अमेरिका
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Mercosur is a trade bloc associated with South America (its members include Brazil, Argentina, Uruguay and Paraguay).

A is incorrect: It is not an Asian bloc.

B is incorrect: It is not a European bloc.

C is incorrect: It is not an African bloc.

💡 Mercosur is a regional common-market grouping in South America.
Q.No 40 EconomyHindi
निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा देश ग्लोबल बिजनेस कॉम्प्लेक्सिटी इंडेक्स 2025 में 79वें स्थान पर है?
A
केमन द्वीपसमूह
✓ Correct
B
नीदरलैंड
C
नॉर्वे
D
मॉरीशस

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Cayman Islands ranked 79th in the Global Business Complexity Index 2025 (a low complexity ranking).

B is incorrect: The Netherlands is not at 79th here.

C is incorrect: Norway is not at 79th.

D is incorrect: Mauritius is not at 79th.

💡 The index ranks jurisdictions by how complex it is to operate a business there.
Q.No 40 EconomyHindi
ग्लोबल ट्रेड आउटलुक एंड स्टैटिस्टिक्स 2025 निम्नलिखित में से किसके द्वारा प्रकाशित की गई थी?
A
यूनेस्को
B
अंतर्राष्ट्रीय मुद्रा कोष
C
विश्व व्यापार संगठन
✓ Correct
D
विश्व बैंक

Solution

Correct answer: C. The 'Global Trade Outlook and Statistics 2025' was published by the World Trade Organization (WTO).

A is incorrect: UNESCO did not publish it.

B is incorrect: The IMF did not publish it.

D is incorrect: The World Bank did not publish it.

💡 The WTO regularly publishes forecasts and statistics on world merchandise and services trade.
Q.No 30 EconomyHindi
99 से पहले किस क्षेत्र को लाइसेंसिंग, कोटा और मूल्य नियंत्रण के माध्यम से भारी विनियमन का सामना करना पड़ा था?
A
कृषि
B
उपभोक्ता और लघु उद्योग
C
भारी उद्योग
✓ Correct
D
केवल बैंकिंग

Solution

Correct answer: C. Before 1991, heavy industry faced heavy regulation through licensing, quotas and price controls (the 'Licence Raj').

A is incorrect: Agriculture was less affected by industrial licensing.

B is incorrect: Consumer/small industry was regulated but heavy industry bore the brunt.

D is incorrect: It was not limited to banking.

💡 The 1991 reforms abolished industrial licensing for most industries.
Q.No 28 EconomyHindi
कौन सी प्रवृत्ति दर्शाती है कि 99 के सुधारों से भारत के बाह्य क्षेत्र की स्थिरता में सुधार हुआ?
A
99 के बाद विदेशी मुद्रा भंडार में लगातार गिरावट
B
990 के दशक में विदेशी ऋण दोगुना हो गया
C
समय के साथ विदेशी मुद्रा भंडार में मजबूत वृद्धि
✓ Correct
D
विदेशी निवेश प्रवाह में गिरावट

Solution

Correct answer: C. The strong, steady rise in India's foreign-exchange reserves over time shows that the 1991 reforms improved external-sector stability.

A is incorrect: Reserves rose, they did not continuously fall, after 1991.

B is incorrect: The aim was to reduce vulnerability, and reserves grew.

D is incorrect: Foreign investment inflows increased, not declined.

💡 In 1991 India's reserves had fallen to just a few weeks of imports, prompting the reforms.
Q.No 32 EconomyEnglish
!५88/२0 (राष्ट्रीय कृषि और ग्रामीण विकास बैंक) की स्थापना कब हुई थी?
A
1969
B
1972
C
1982
✓ Correct
D
1990

Solution

Correct answer: C. NABARD (National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development) was established in 1982.

A is incorrect: 1969 was the year of bank nationalization.

B is incorrect: 1972 is incorrect.

D is incorrect: 1990 is incorrect.

💡 NABARD is the apex development bank for agriculture and the rural economy.
Q.No 34 EconomyHindi
हाल ही में कौन सा देश भारत का सबसे बड़ा व्यापारिक साझेदार रहा है?
A
संयुक्त राज्य अमेरिका
B
जनवादी गणराज्य चीन
✓ Correct
C
संयुक्त अरब अमीरात
D
जापान

Solution

Correct answer: B. In recent years, the People's Republic of China has been India's largest trading partner (by total trade).

A is incorrect: The USA is among the top partners but China has often led in total trade.

C is incorrect: The UAE is a major partner but not the largest overall.

D is incorrect: Japan is a significant partner but not the largest.

💡 India runs a large trade deficit with China, importing far more than it exports.
Q.No 39 EconomyEnglish
"कृषि व्यवसाय के अवसर एवं चुनौतियों में महिलाएं" रिपोर्ट के अनुसार, 2025 में भारत के कृषि कार्यबल का कितना प्रतिशत महिलाओं द्वारा गठित किया जाएगा?
A
64.4%
✓ Correct
B
51.4%
C
45.4%
D
54.4%

Solution

Correct answer: A. As per the cited report, women constitute about 64.4% of India's agricultural workforce.

B is incorrect: 51.4% understates the figure.

C is incorrect: 45.4% is incorrect.

D is incorrect: 54.4% is not the figure.

💡 Women play a major role in Indian agriculture, especially in sowing, weeding and harvesting.
Q.No 26 EconomyHindi
990 के दशक के आरंभ में किस बाह्म घटना ने भारत के भुगतान संतुलन संकट को और बदतर बना दिया?
A
एशियाई वित्तीय संकट
B
खाड़ी युद्ध और बढ़ती तेल कीमतें
✓ Correct
C
सोवियत संघ का पतन
D
कृषि निर्यात में गिरावट

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Gulf War and the resulting rise in oil prices worsened India's balance-of-payments crisis in the early 1990s.

A is incorrect: The Asian financial crisis came later (1997).

C is incorrect: The collapse of the USSR contributed but the immediate trigger was the Gulf War oil shock.

D is incorrect: A fall in agricultural exports was not the main external cause.

💡 The 1991 crisis forced India to pledge gold and adopt sweeping economic reforms.
Q.No 29 EconomyHindi
भारत में, हरित क्रांति मुख्य रूप से को संदर्भित करती हैं।
A
खाद्यान्न उत्पादन में भारी वृद्धि
✓ Correct
B
देश के हरित आवरण क्षेत्र में भारी वृद्धि
C
बागवानी उत्पादन में भारी वृद्धि
D
झींगा पालन के उत्पादन में भारी वृद्धि

Solution

Correct answer: A. In India, the Green Revolution refers mainly to a huge increase in food-grain production (especially wheat and rice).

B is incorrect: It was not about increasing green (forest) cover.

C is incorrect: It was not chiefly about horticulture.

D is incorrect: Shrimp farming (Blue Revolution) is separate.

💡 The Green Revolution used HYV seeds, fertilizers, irrigation and machinery.
Q.No 27 EconomyHindi
चौथी पंचवर्षीय योजना (969-74) के दौरान, केंद्रीय औद्योगिक उद्देश्यों में से एक.__ था।
A
केवल उपभोक्ता वस्तुओं में आयात प्रतिस्थापन
B
विदेश व्यापार नीतियों का उदारीकरण
C
आईटी और सेवा उद्योगों को प्राथमिकता देना
D
भारी उद्योगों को मजबूत करने और विदेशी सहायता को कम करने के माध्यम से आत्मनिर्भरता
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. During the Fourth Five-Year Plan (1969-74), a central industrial objective was self-reliance through strengthening heavy industry and reducing dependence on foreign aid.

A is incorrect: It was not limited to import substitution in consumer goods only.

B is incorrect: It was not about liberalizing foreign trade.

C is incorrect: IT/services were not prioritised in that era.

💡 The Fourth Plan's themes were 'growth with stability' and self-reliance.
Q.No 31 EconomyHindi
दामोदर घाटी निगम (0४८) की स्थापना के लिए की गई थी।
A
व्यापार संवर्धन
B
इस्पात उत्पादन
C
बहुउद्देशीय नदी घाटी विकास
✓ Correct
D
ग्रामीण ऋण

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Damodar Valley Corporation (DVC) was established for multipurpose river-valley development (flood control, irrigation, power).

A is incorrect: It was not set up for trade promotion.

B is incorrect: It was not for steel production.

D is incorrect: It was not for rural credit.

💡 The DVC (1948) was India's first multipurpose river-valley project, modelled on the TVA of the USA.
Q.No 33 EconomyHindi
भारत की विदेश व्यापार नीति के निर्माण और कार्यान्वयन का दायित्व किस संस्था को सौंपा गया है?
A
भारतीय रिजर्व बैंक (आरबीआई)
B
नीति आयोग
C
विदेश व्यापार महानिदेशालय (डीजीएफटी)
✓ Correct
D
गृह मंत्रालय

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) is responsible for formulating and implementing India's Foreign Trade Policy.

A is incorrect: The RBI handles monetary policy and forex management.

B is incorrect: NITI Aayog is a policy think-tank.

D is incorrect: The Ministry of Home Affairs is unrelated to trade policy.

💡 The DGFT functions under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry.
Q.No 24 EconomyHindi
राजस्व संग्रहण को मजबूत करने के लिए 99 के बाद भारत में कौन सा प्रमुख राजकोषीय सुधार लागू किया गया?
A
सब्सिडी में वृद्धि
B
वस्तु एवं सेवा कर (जीएसटी) का परिचय
C
मॉड वैट (4000//#0) के माध्यम से अप्रत्यक्ष करों को सरल बनाना
✓ Correct
D
आयकर को पूरी तरह से हटाना

Solution

Correct answer: C. A major post-1991 fiscal reform to strengthen revenue collection was the simplification of indirect taxes through MODVAT/VAT (moving towards a value-added tax system that reduced cascading).

A is incorrect: Increasing subsidies does not strengthen revenue.

B is incorrect: GST came much later (2017); the post-1991 reform referred to here is the MODVAT/VAT simplification.

D is incorrect: Income tax was not abolished.

💡 MODVAT/CENVAT and later state VAT (2005) progressively reduced the cascading of indirect taxes, culminating in GST (2017).
Q.No 27 EconomyHindi
भारत का पहला निर्यात प्रसंस्करण क्षेत्र (ई.पी.जेड.) कहां स्थापित किया गया था?
A
पारादीप
B
कांडला
✓ Correct
C
मुंद्रा
D
हल्दिया

Solution

Correct answer: B. India's first Export Processing Zone (EPZ) was set up at Kandla, Gujarat (1965).

A is incorrect: Paradip was not the first EPZ.

C is incorrect: Mundra was not the first EPZ.

D is incorrect: Haldia was not the first EPZ.

💡 Kandla was also Asia's first Export Processing Zone.
Q.No 28 EconomyHindi
भारत के 99 के बाह्म क्षेत्र सुधारों में व्यापार उदारीकरण की दिशा में एक कदम शामिल था। कौन सा उपाय इस नीतिगत बदलाव का सबसे अच्छा उदाहरण है?
A
घरेलू उद्योगों की सुरक्षा के लिए उच्च टैरिफ लगाना
B
अधिकांश वस्तुओं के लिए आयात लाइसेंस प्रणाली को समाप्त करना
✓ Correct
C
आयात पर मात्रात्मक प्रतिबंध बढ़ाना
D
फॉरेन एक्सचेंज रिज़र्व को कंट्रोल करने के लिए एक्सपोर्ट कोटा तय करना

Solution

Correct answer: B. A key step in India's post-1991 trade liberalization was abolishing the import-licensing system for most goods (ending the 'Licence Raj' in trade).

A is incorrect: Imposing high tariffs is protectionist, not liberalizing.

C is incorrect: Increasing quantitative restrictions is the opposite of liberalization.

D is incorrect: Fixing export quotas is not liberalization.

💡 The 1991 reforms removed licensing and reduced tariffs to open up the economy.
Q.No 30 EconomyEnglish
भारत का पहला आर्थिक सर्वेक्षण में प्रस्तुत किया गया था।
A
1947-48
B
1950-51
✓ Correct
C
1951-52
D
1952-53

Solution

Correct answer: B. India's first Economic Survey was presented for 1950-51.

A is incorrect: 1947-48 is too early.

C is incorrect: 1951-52 is not the first.

D is incorrect: 1952-53 is incorrect.

💡 From 1964, the Economic Survey began to be presented separately, just before the Union Budget.
Q.No 31 EconomyHindi
भारत डाक ग्रामीण अर्थव्यवस्था के लिए उत्प्रेरक के रूप में कैसे कार्य करता है?
A
डिजिटल सेवाएँ और माइक्रोक्रेडिट विस्तार
✓ Correct
B
जल आपूर्ति
C
स्वास्थ्य बीमा
D
दूरसंचार सेवाएं

Solution

Correct answer: A. India Post acts as a catalyst for the rural economy through digital services and microcredit/financial-inclusion expansion (e.g. the India Post Payments Bank).

B is incorrect: It does not provide water supply.

C is incorrect: It is not primarily a health-insurance provider.

D is incorrect: Telecom services are not its main rural-economy role here.

💡 The India Post Payments Bank extends banking to remote rural areas.
Q.No 33 EconomyEnglish
भारत में, जन धन खातों की संख्या जून 2025 तक बढ़कर करोड़ हो गई है।
A
42.94 crore
B
22.94 crore
C
25.22 crore
D
55.22 crore
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. By June 2025, the number of Jan Dhan accounts had grown to about 55.22 crore.

A is incorrect: 42.94 crore understates the figure.

B is incorrect: 22.94 crore is far too low.

C is incorrect: 25.22 crore is incorrect.

💡 The Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (2014) drove financial inclusion through zero-balance accounts.
Q.No 21 EconomyEnglish
Which sector was given top priority in India’s Second Five-Year Plan?
A
Agriculture
B
Heavy industry
✓ Correct
C
Services
D
Small-scale enterprises

Solution

Correct answer: B. India's Second Five-Year Plan (the Mahalanobis Plan) gave top priority to heavy industry.

A is incorrect: Agriculture was the focus of the First Plan.

C is incorrect: Services were not the priority then.

D is incorrect: Small-scale enterprises were supported but not the top priority.

💡 The Second Plan set up the public-sector steel plants at Bhilai, Rourkela and Durgapur.
Q.No 22 EconomyEnglish
The concept of five year plans of India was borrowed from :
A
United Kingdome
B
United states of America
C
France
D
Former Soviet Union
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The concept of Five-Year Plans was borrowed from the former Soviet Union.

A is incorrect: It was not borrowed from the UK.

B is incorrect: It was not from the USA.

C is incorrect: It was not from France.

💡 The Planning Commission was set up in 1950 to formulate India's Five-Year Plans.
Q.No 24 EconomyHindi
हाल की रिपोर्टों में, कौन सा क्षेत्र आमतौर पर खुशी सूचकांक पर सबसे अधिक अंक प्राप्त करता है?
A
उप-सहारा अफ्रीका
B
मध्य पूर्व
C
नॉर्डिक देश (उत्तरी यूरोप)
✓ Correct
D
दक्षिण एशिया

Solution

Correct answer: C. In recent happiness reports, the Nordic countries (northern Europe) consistently score the highest.

A is incorrect: Sub-Saharan Africa scores low on the index.

B is incorrect: The Middle East does not top the index.

D is incorrect: South Asia scores relatively low.

💡 Finland has topped the World Happiness Report for several years running.
Q.No 29 EconomyHindi
प्रसिद्ध "हिंदू विकास दर" वाक्यांश किसके द्वारा गढ़ा गया था?
A
मोंटेक सिंह अहलूवालिया
B
राज कृष्ण
✓ Correct
C
जगदीश भगवती
D
अमर्त्य सेन

Solution

Correct answer: B. The phrase 'Hindu rate of growth' was coined by the economist Raj Krishna.

A is incorrect: Montek Singh Ahluwalia did not coin it.

C is incorrect: Jagdish Bhagwati did not coin it.

D is incorrect: Amartya Sen did not coin it.

💡 The term described India's low (~3-4%) growth rate from the 1950s to the 1980s.
Q.No 30 EconomyHindi
भारत में मुख्य मुद्रास्फीति को मापने के लिए किस सूचकांक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
A
संयुक्त उपभोक्ता मूल्य सूचकांक
✓ Correct
B
सकल घरेलू उत्पाद अपस्फीतक
C
औद्योगिक उत्पादन सूचकांक
D
थोक मूल्य सूचकांक

Solution

Correct answer: A. India uses the Combined Consumer Price Index (CPI) to measure headline (retail) inflation for monetary-policy targeting.

B is incorrect: The GDP deflator is a broad measure but not the headline inflation gauge used.

C is incorrect: The Index of Industrial Production measures output, not inflation.

D is incorrect: The Wholesale Price Index measures wholesale, not retail, prices.

💡 The RBI targets CPI inflation at 4% (with a band of +/- 2%).
Q.No 27 EconomyEnglish
Which statement best captures the industrial performance of India during the Third Five-Year Plan (1961-66)?
A
The plan failed due to wars and droughts, leading to import dependence
✓ Correct
B
Industry grew as expected, while agriculture stagnated
C
