Industrial output doubled despite fiscal imbalances
D
The Plan marked the beginning of privatization and liberalization

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Third Five-Year Plan (1961-66) is regarded as a failure because wars (with China in 1962 and Pakistan in 1965) and droughts forced India into food and import dependence.

B is incorrect: Industry did not simply grow as planned; the plan was disrupted.

C is incorrect: Industrial output did not double.

D is incorrect: Liberalization/privatization began much later.

💡 After the Third Plan, India had a three-year 'plan holiday'.
Q.No 29 EconomyEnglish
The Fifth Five-Year Plan (1974-1979) focused on:
A
Trade liberalization
B
Export-oriented growth
C
Poverty alleviation and employment generation
✓ Correct
D
Industrialization only

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Fifth Five-Year Plan (1974-79) focused on poverty alleviation ('Garibi Hatao') and employment generation.

A is incorrect: Trade liberalization came later (1991).

B is incorrect: Export-oriented growth was not its focus.

D is incorrect: It was not about industrialization only.

💡 The Minimum Needs Programme was launched during the Fifth Plan.
Q.No 26 EconomyHindi
योजना काल के दौरान, भारत में आयात प्रतिस्थापन औद्योगीकरण (आई एसआई) का उद्देश्य क्या था?
A
विदेशी मुद्रा अर्जन के लिए निर्मित वस्तुओं के निर्यात को प्रोत्साहित करना
B
उपभोक्ता वस्तु उद्योगों में विदेशी निवेश आकर्षित करना
C
संरक्षणवादी नीतियों के माध्यम से निर्मित उत्पादों के आयात को घरेलू उत्पादन से प्रतिस्थापित करना
✓ Correct
D
मशीनरी और पूंजीगत वस्तुओं के आयात को बिना किसी प्रतिबंध के उदार बनाया जाए

Solution

Correct answer: C. Import Substitution Industrialisation (ISI) aimed to replace imported manufactured goods with domestic production through protectionist policies.

A is incorrect: It was not about promoting exports (that is export-led growth).

B is incorrect: It was not about attracting foreign investment.

D is incorrect: It was not about liberalizing imports.

💡 ISI was the dominant strategy in India from the 1950s until the 1991 reforms.
Q.No 28 EconomyEnglish
भारत में एफडीआई (प्रत्यक्ष विदेशी निवेश) की सीमा को कब उदार बनाया गया?
A
1991
✓ Correct
B
1995
C
2000
D
2010

Solution

Correct answer: A. FDI (Foreign Direct Investment) limits in India were liberalised in 1991 as part of the economic reforms.

B is incorrect: 1995 is not when the major liberalization began.

C is incorrect: 2000 is later.

D is incorrect: 2010 is much later.

💡 The 1991 reforms opened many sectors to foreign investment.
Q.No 29 EconomyHindi
सूक्ष्म,लघु और मध्यम उद्यमों (5+4६5) और स्टार्टअप्स के लिए क्रेडिट गारंटी फंड का उद्देश्य कया है?
A
संपार्श्चिक-मुक्त गारंटी समर्थन प्रदान करना
✓ Correct
B
अनुसंधान के लिए अनुदान देना
C
पेंशन कोष
D
गृह ऋण सुरक्षा

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Credit Guarantee Fund for MSMEs and startups aims to provide collateral-free guarantee support for loans.

B is incorrect: It is not a research grant.

C is incorrect: It is not a pension fund.

D is incorrect: It is not home-loan security.

💡 The CGTMSE scheme enables banks to lend to small enterprises without collateral.
Q.No 23 EconomyHindi
विश्व प्रसन्नता रिपोर्ट का क्या उद्देश्य है?
A
अकेले गरीबी पर नज़र रखें
B
केवल विकासशील देशों को रैंक करें
C
निवेश निर्णयों के लिए डेटा प्रदान करें
D
मानव कल्याण को मापें और मानव सुख के लिए नीति का मार्गदर्शन करें
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The World Happiness Report aims to measure human well-being and guide policy towards greater human happiness.

A is incorrect: It tracks more than just poverty.

B is incorrect: It ranks all countries, not just developing ones.

C is incorrect: It is not designed for investment decisions.

💡 It uses six factors including GDP per capita, social support and freedom of choice.
Q.No 28 EconomyHindi
नरसिम्हम समिति द्वारा अनुशंसित बैंकिंग क्षेत्र सुधारों (99) में क्या शामिल था?
A
पूंजी पर्याप्तता मानदंड
B
ब्याज दर विनियमन
C
गैर-निष्पादित परिसंपत्तियों ((/8) में कमी
D
ये सभी
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The Narasimham Committee (1991) banking reforms included all of these - capital adequacy norms, interest-rate deregulation and reduction of non-performing assets.

A is incorrect: Capital adequacy norms were one recommendation, not the only one.

B is incorrect: Interest-rate deregulation was one of several.

C is incorrect: NPA reduction was one of several.

💡 The Narasimham Committee reports (1991 and 1998) shaped India's banking-sector reforms.
Q.No 30 EconomyHindi
“मुद्रास्फीतिजनित मंदी” किसकी स्थिति है?
A
मुद्रास्फीति + व्यापार अधिशेष
B
मुद्रास्फीति + रोजगार में वृद्धि
C
मुद्रास्फीति + बढ़ता सकल घरेलू उत्पाद
D
मुद्रास्फीति + बेरोजगारी
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. 'Stagflation' is the situation of high inflation combined with high unemployment (and stagnant growth).

A is incorrect: It is not inflation with a trade surplus.

B is incorrect: It is not inflation with rising employment.

C is incorrect: It is not inflation with rising GDP.

💡 Stagflation is hard to tackle because fixing inflation and unemployment require opposite policies.
Q.No 27 EconomyHindi
किस पंचवर्षीय योजना को “गाडगिल योजना” कहा जाता है?
A
पहली योजना
B
दूसरी योजना
C
तीसरी योजना
✓ Correct
D
चौथी योजना

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Third Five-Year Plan is also called the 'Gadgil Yojana' (after D.R. Gadgil's role in planning).

A is incorrect: The First Plan is not the Gadgil Yojana.

B is incorrect: The Second Plan is the Mahalanobis Plan.

D is incorrect: The Fourth Plan is not called the Gadgil Yojana.

💡 The Third Plan (1961-66) aimed at a self-reliant, self-generating economy.
Q.No 28 EconomyEnglish
भुगतान संतुलन संकट से निपटने के लिए जुलाई 99 में भारतीय रुपये का कितना अवमूल्यन किया गया था?
A
5-7%
B
18-20%
✓ Correct
C
30-35%
D
50%

Solution

Correct answer: B. In July 1991, the Indian rupee was devalued by about 18-20% (in two steps) to address the balance-of-payments crisis.

A is incorrect: 5-7% understates the devaluation.

C is incorrect: 30-35% overstates it.

D is incorrect: 50% is far too high.

💡 The 1991 devaluation was part of the broader economic reform package.
Q.No 29 EconomyEnglish
भारतीय एक्सज़िम बैंक की स्थापना कब हुई थी?
A
1972
B
1982
✓ Correct
C
1985
D
1990

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Export-Import (EXIM) Bank of India was established in 1982.

A is incorrect: 1972 is incorrect.

C is incorrect: 1985 is incorrect.

D is incorrect: 1990 is incorrect.

💡 EXIM Bank finances and promotes India's foreign trade.
Q.No 32 EconomyHindi
निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा प्रवाह चर है?
A
राष्ट्रीय आय
✓ Correct
B
पैसे की आपूर्ति
C
शेयर पूंजी
D
विदेशी मुद्रा भंडार

Solution

Correct answer: A. National income is a flow variable - it is measured over a period of time (e.g. a year).

B is incorrect: Money supply is a stock variable (measured at a point in time).

C is incorrect: Share capital is a stock variable.

D is incorrect: Foreign-exchange reserves are a stock variable.

💡 A flow is measured per unit time; a stock is measured at a moment in time.
Q.No 33 EconomyHindi
भारत की हरित क्रांति के निर्माता के रूप में किसे जाना जाता है और 2024 में भारत रत्न से सम्मानित किया गया?
A
वर्गीस कुरियन
B
एम. एस. स्वामीनाथन
✓ Correct
C
नॉर्मन बोरलॉग
D
सी. सुब्रमण्यम

Solution

Correct answer: B. M.S. Swaminathan is known as the architect of India's Green Revolution and was awarded the Bharat Ratna in 2024.

A is incorrect: Verghese Kurien led the White (milk) Revolution.

C is incorrect: Norman Borlaug led the global Green Revolution but is not Indian.

D is incorrect: C. Subramaniam was the agriculture minister who supported it, but the 'architect' title is Swaminathan's.

💡 M.S. Swaminathan was posthumously conferred the Bharat Ratna in 2024.
Q.No 40 EconomyEnglish
वैश्विक प्रेषण अंतर्वाह रिपोर्ट के अनुसार, भारत को 2024 में वैश्विक प्रेषणों का प्राप्त हुआ था।
A
8.2%
B
14.3%
✓ Correct
C
18.1%
D
25.3%

Solution

Correct answer: B. According to the global remittance-inflow data, India received about 14.3% of global remittances in 2024 - the largest share of any country.

A is incorrect: 8.2% understates India's share.

C is incorrect: 18.1% overstates it.

D is incorrect: 25.3% is far too high.

💡 India has been the world's top recipient of remittances for many years.
